execution_prompt.scope: Single milestone: M3 — Add `is_thinking: bool` field to StreamToken dataclass and propagate through IPC protocol

git_state.head: 74906f1
git_state.working_tree: CLEAN (ignore phase2_gates/evidence/uat2_real_runtime_activation.json — known live-run artifact, do not commit)

step.name: branch
step.action: Create and checkout a new branch from main HEAD (74906f1).

step.name: streamtoken_dataclass
step.action: Add `is_thinking: bool` field to the `StreamToken` frozen dataclass in `services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py`.
step.details:
Current fields (lines 81-85): token: str token_index: int is_final: bool is_tool_call: bool session_id: str
Add AFTER `is_tool_call`: is_thinking: bool = False
The default `False` ensures backward compatibility — existing StreamToken constructions that omit the field will work without modification (frozen dataclass positional issue: since `session_id: str` has no default, `is_thinking` MUST either go after `session_id` or `session_id` must be given a default. Preferred approach: place `is_thinking: bool = False` AFTER `session_id: str` to avoid breaking positional construction patterns).
Update the docstring to document the new field: is_thinking: True if this token is part of an internal chain-of-thought block (thinking mode). The TUI may render these differently or hide them.

step.name: serialization
step.details:
to_dict(): Add `"is_thinking": self.is_thinking` to the returned dict.
from_dict(): Add `is_thinking=bool(data.get("is_thinking", False))` to the constructor call. The `False` default in from_dict ensures backward compatibility with messages from older Orchestrator versions that don't include the field.

step.name: ipc_protocol
step.action: Update `encode_stream_token()` in `shared/ipc/protocol.py` (line ~332) to accept and include `is_thinking`.
step.details: Add parameter: `is_thinking: bool = False` to the method signature. Add `"is_thinking": is_thinking` to the payload dict. The default=False ensures all existing callers continue to work without modification.

step.name: ao_entrypoint_producer
step.action: Update the AO entrypoint (`services/assistant_orchestrator/src/entrypoint.py`) stream token emission to pass `is_thinking=False` explicitly.
step.details:
There are 3 call sites for `encode_stream_token()` in this file (lines ~757, ~804, ~818). All should pass `is_thinking=False` explicitly for clarity. The AO's Streamer already suppresses thinking tokens from the callback (M2 implementation), so at the IPC level all emitted tokens are non-thinking tokens. This is a documentation/explicitness change only.
NOTE: If in the future the AO wants to forward thinking tokens to the TUI (e.g., for a collapsed thinking panel), it would set `is_thinking=True` on those tokens. That is NOT in scope for M3 — M3 only adds the field and wiring. The AO Streamer suppression from M2 remains the active thinking-token filtering mechanism.

step.name: unit_tests_transport
step.action: Update existing and add new tests in `services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py`.
step.details:
1. Update `_make_token()` helper (or equivalent `TestStreamToken._make_token`) to include `is_thinking` in defaults dict (default False).
2. Update `test_construction`: assert `t.is_thinking is False`.
3. Update `test_to_dict`: assert `"is_thinking": False` in the dict.
4. Update `test_round_trip`: should pass naturally if to_dict/from_dict are correct.
5. Update `test_from_dict_defaults`: assert `t.is_thinking is False` for empty dict.
6. Add NEW test `test_is_thinking_true_round_trip`: - Construct StreamToken with is_thinking=True - Verify to_dict includes `"is_thinking": True` - Verify from_dict round-trip preserves the value
7. Add NEW test `test_is_thinking_default_false`: - Construct StreamToken without passing is_thinking (positional args only + session_id) - Verify is_thinking is False

step.name: unit_tests_streaming
step.details: The helper constructs StreamToken with keyword args. If `is_thinking` has a default value, this helper should continue to work without changes. Verify by running the tests. If the field is positional (no default), update the helper to pass `is_thinking=False`.

step.name: integration_tests
step.details:
There are ~8 direct StreamToken() calls using positional args (lines 344, 345, 351, 415, 417). If `is_thinking` is placed AFTER `session_id` with a default, these should work as-is. Verify all pass. If any fail due to positional arg issues, update to use keyword `session_id=` or add `is_thinking=False` explicitly.
Also verify that `encode_stream_token()` calls in the test handlers work — they use positional args too. The new `is_thinking=False` default on encode_stream_token should handle this.

step.name: test_gate
step.action: Run the full test suite TWICE and capture results.
step.command: Run 1: python -m pytest --tb=short -q --no-header 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 10 Run 2: python -m pytest --tb=short -q --no-header 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 10
step.gate: Both runs must show 784+ passed, 0 failures. Test count may increase due to new tests added in step 6.

step.name: mypy_check
step.action: Run mypy on changed files to verify type correctness.
step.gate: Zero type errors on these files (existing `import-untyped` suppressions are expected/acceptable).

step.name: commit
step.action: Stage and commit changed files only. Do NOT stage phase2_gates/evidence/uat2_real_runtime_activation.json.
step.command: git add services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py shared/ipc/protocol.py services/assistant_orchestrator/src/entrypoint.py services/ui_gateway/tests/test_transport.py services/ui_shell/tests/test_streaming.py tests/integration/test_p114_ui_end_to_end.py git commit -m "feat(transport): add StreamToken.is_thinking field for thinking mode (ADR-012 §2.4 M3)"
step.note: Only add files you actually modified. If test_streaming.py or test_p114_ui_end_to_end.py were not changed, do not stage them.

step.name: ff_merge
step.action: Fast-forward merge the feature branch to main.
step.command: git checkout main git merge --ff-only feature/p5-m3-streamtoken-is-thinking git rev-parse --short HEAD

step.name: deliverables
step.action: Report the following to the Lead Architect:
step.checklist: - Final HEAD SHA on main - Test count (collected / passed) from both runs - Number of new tests added - Files modified (list) - mypy result (pass/fail) - Any unexpected issues encountered

constraints.constraint: Do NOT modify docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md — that is Task 3b scope.
Do NOT modify docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md — governance doc updates are separate.
Do NOT alter M1 or M2 logic in policy_agent or assistant_orchestrator inference files.
The AO Streamer suppression (M2) remains the active thinking-token filter. M3 adds field+wiring only.
Maintain backward compatibility: all existing StreamToken constructions must work without modification (use default=False).
Stop after commit + merge. Do not start unrequested work.

execution_prompt.architectural_context:
ADR-012 §2.4 Thinking Mode Strategy (LOCKED): - M1 (PA /no_think + dual stop tokens): COMPLETE (601eb71 + d452003 + add0a05) - M2 (AO thinking allowed + block strip + streamer suppression): COMPLETE (155ea61) - M3 (StreamToken.is_thinking field): THIS MILESTONE
M3 is the smallest of the three thinking mode milestones. It adds the is_thinking boolean to the transport layer so the TUI CAN distinguish thinking tokens from visible tokens. Currently the AO Streamer suppresses thinking tokens entirely (M2), so all tokens reaching the gateway are is_thinking=False. This field enables future TUI rendering options (e.g., collapsed thinking panel) without requiring further transport layer changes.
execution_prompt.commit_template: feat(transport): add StreamToken.is_thinking field for thinking mode (ADR-012 §2.4 M3)
